model.note: 25% fee-switch GP on LP fees + 0.30bps frontend fee; volume bootstrapped from 2021+ monthly history

secondary_chart.label: Historical Mcap / GP (full activation)
secondary_chart.subtitle: Market cap ÷ annualised full-activation GP
secondary_chart.note: Rolling 30D volume & fee rate (no lookahead). Full activation = 25% LP fees + 0.30bps frontend.

model.note: Card GP = GDV × 135bps take × margin; staking GP = ETH bootstrap; vault flat

secondary_chart.label: Historical Mcap / GP (TVL-anchored proxy)
secondary_chart.subtitle: Market cap ÷ TVL × current GP/TVL rate
secondary_chart.note: TVL-anchored GP proxy; card business growth is not captured historically.

model.note: 50% of entity GP used for buybacks; 0 scheduled unlocks until Jupiter announces reserve plan

secondary_chart.label: Historical perps GP buyback horizon
secondary_chart.subtitle: Years to buy circ supply at 50% of annualised perps GP
secondary_chart.note: Uses trailing 30D JUP Perps fees × 25% take × 50% buyback share. Perps GP only; spot GP excluded.

data.name: Hyperliquid

model.note: HL vol × Binance market share × 0.026% clean treasury revenue take-rate + USDC yield; MS30/MS90 momentum decays over 12 months; 4 supply/emission scenarios

mcp_bullets: Protocol fee revenue accelerated versus the 180D annualised run rate.
Hyperliquid Perps ranked #1 by 30D derivatives volume in the DefiLlama derivatives category snapshot.
30D market share (16.2%) is running 25% above the 180D baseline (13.0%), signalling momentum acceleration.
Rolling 30D/Binance share grew 4.8pp over the last 90D window (current MS30 16.2%).
At spot, clean-revenue-only buyback horizon is 32.9Y (trailing 30D clean treasury revenue / target supply at spot price).
caveats: Market share (MS90) from DefiLlama MCP derivatives volume vs scaled Binance BTCUSDT proxy.
USDC yield modeled separately from perp revenue; TVL follows HL volume path via estimated elasticity.
Supply scenarios range from DB-observed emissions (~1M/mo) to worst-case non-circulating overhang release.

data.name: Sky

model.note: GP = gross income - savings-rate cost - stUSDS expense. NP = GP - OPEX. USDS growth path: money-market TVL returns × 0.65 dampener, capped ±8%/+10% monthly. DAI supply flat. Treasury = cumulative positive NP. Protocol financials locked 2026-05-08.

caveats: Protocol financials (gross income, savings rate, USDS/DAI supply) locked at 2026-05-08; re-run sky_data_collection to refresh.
USDS growth is modeled on broad money-market TVL returns dampened by 0.65 — Sky may diverge from sector.
No buybacks or SKY supply reduction modeled; treasury accumulates cash only.
Savings rate (3.65%) and USDS savings penetration (81.2%) are point-in-time inputs.

secondary_chart.label: Historical Mcap / GP (supply-anchored proxy)
secondary_chart.subtitle: Market cap ÷ (USDS+DAI supply × net GP/supply rate)
secondary_chart.note: GP proxy = (USDS+DAI supply) × yield × (1 − savings penetration × savings rate). Fixed rates.

data.name: Lighter

model.note: Lighter perps volume is anchored to Binance Futures market share, using MS90 as the starting valuation seed; holder revenue funds 100% buybacks; fixed post-cliff supply unlocks are included per path. Stablecoin yield is shown as a sensitivity, not confirmed current Lighter treasury revenue.

caveats: Lighter paid derivatives history is short; historical diagnostics use daily price and trailing 30D revenue where available.
Market-share trend uses rolling-window snapshots (30D/90D/180D) against Binance Futures, not a full daily share history.
Stablecoin yield is a sensitivity copied from the HYPE framework and should be treated as optional future business, not current Lighter protocol revenue.
P90 and chart distribution are approximated from MC P25/P50/P75 because the imported Lighter run exported quartiles rather than the full terminal distribution.

secondary_chart.label: Historical buyback horizon
secondary_chart.subtitle: Market cap divided by trailing 30D holder revenue annualized
secondary_chart.note: Supply-adjusted MC buyback years are shown in the snapshot table; this historical chart uses observed market cap and trailing holder revenue only.

mcp_bullets: 30D paid perps volume: $44.4B, equal to 3.05% of the Binance Futures proxy.
Trailing 30D revenue annualizes to $33.7M; holder revenue capture is 97.6%.
Primary P50 PV uses holder revenue plus HYPE-style TVL yield sensitivity and fixed token unlock schedule through month 36.

data.name: Venice Token

model.note: Year-3 platform revenue (subscription + API) × 15× / (1.25)³ / Y3 supply. Revenue is estimated from observed VVV subscription burn ratios (~9% capture); on-chain DefiLlama burns ($3M ann.) are the only directly measured component. Three manual scenarios replace Monte Carlo; distributions approximate log-normal σ=1.0.

caveats: Venice does not publicly disclose platform revenue. The ~$32M/yr estimate is derived from observed subscription VVV burns (~$150K/month in April 2026) ÷ implied tier burn rate (~9%) and is not confirmed by Venice.
On-chain DefiLlama fees ($250K/30D, $3M ann.) capture only VVV buyback-and-burn spend — not total subscription or API revenue.
VVV has no max supply cap. Long-run supply depends on the emission schedule (currently 3M VVV/yr, stepped down from 14M) and organic burn rate.
Scenarios are manually constructed with no Monte Carlo simulation. Distributions approximate log-normal σ=1.0 for comparability with other dashboard tokens.
The 15× revenue multiple is applied for cross-token comparability; it is aggressive for a SaaS/AI platform and implies strong long-run growth is required to justify current prices.
mcp_bullets: Trailing 30D on-chain VVV burns (DefiLlama): $250K, annualizing to $3.0M — the only directly measured holder revenue metric (supply reduction via buyback-and-burn).
Estimated total platform revenue ~$32M/yr, derived from April 2026 subscription burn rates ($150K/mo) × implied ~9% capture ratio; actual revenue is off-chain and undisclosed.
Annual VVV emissions cut to 3M/yr from July 2026 (down from 14M at launch); 3Y gross issuance ~9M tokens. Net supply change depends on burn rate growth vs. emissions.
